data.title: Mainland Regulators Question Air China, China Southern Over Ticket Overselling
data.description:
Mainland media reported that consumer associations in Beijing, Tianjin and surrounding areas launched a joint special survey on awareness of civil aviation ticket overselling. The groups held joint talks today with 10 airlines, including Air China (0753) and China Southern Airlines (1055), as well as five online ticketing platforms, including Tongcheng (0780), Trip.com (9961) and Meituan (3690), according to Ming Pao.

The companies were asked to complete their self-inspection and rectification work by the 31st of next month.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces $1.47 Billion Short Liquidations Above $68,421
data.description: Bitcoin could trigger $1.47 billion in short liquidations on major centralized exchanges if it breaks above $68,421. According to ChainCatcher, long liquidations on major CEXs would total $1.37 billion if BTC falls below $62,211.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Kyiv Strikes Hang Over Russia Rate Decision
data.description: Russian businesses are facing their toughest conditions since the early months of the war in Ukraine, according to Bloomberg. The strain from Kyiv’s strikes is complicating the central bank’s decision on whether to pause its yearlong easing cycle, a move that would keep borrowing costs elevated longer.

data.title: Puyuan Stock Slips After Forecasting 1925% Jump in First-Half Profit
data.description:
Puyuan Semiconductor (688766.SH) fell more than 4% on July 24 after issuing a strong first-half 2026 earnings forecast, with its market value at about 58 billion yuan, according to Jiemian News. The company said revenue for the first six months was expected to reach about 3.95 billion yuan, up about 335.65% from a year earlier, while attributable net profit was forecast at about 825 million yuan, up about 1,925.36%, and adjusted net profit at about 820 million yuan, up about 2,976.99%.

Puyuan said the gains were driven by a stronger storage-chip market as global AI compute buildout lifted demand and prices, while its “storage-plus” strategy helped MCU and driver products expand into industrial control and AIoT applications. It also said the acquisition in November 2025 of a 51% stake in Zhuhai Noah Changtian Storage Technology and the consolidation of its wholly owned subsidiary Skyhigh Memory Limited added to revenue and profit in the reporting period. The company said research and development, management, and sales expenses rose by about 240 million yuan in total.

Separately, Puyuan said on July 20 that its chairman and controlling shareholder Wang Nan proposed a share buyback of 30 million yuan to 50 million yuan, to be carried out through centralized bidding. The repurchased shares would be sold 12 months after the buyback result announcement and cancelled if unsold after three years. Puyuan's stock had risen more than 500% at one point this year and hit an intraday record of 909.8 yuan per share on July 1 before pulling back.

data.title: European Union’s Top Diplomat Kallas Says She Did Not Expect to Be Included in New U.S. Tariffs
data.description: European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said she did not expect to be included in the new U.S. tariffs. According to Jin10, EU officials will seek clarification from Washington on the new tariff issue.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Jobless Claims Fall to 187,000 as Brent Returns to $100
data.description:
Initial jobless claims plunged to 187,000, and Brent crude returned to $100. According to Jin10, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield rose to 4.71%, a new high for the year.

According to Jin10, the probability of a European Central Bank rate hike in September has exceeded 50%, and the euro fell in a "buy the rumor, sell the fact" move.

data.title: STOCKS | Korea Future Asset Increases Strategy Holdings by 25,573 Shares
data.description: Korea Future Asset increased its holdings of Strategy stock by 25,573 shares, according to BitcoinTreasuries.NET monitoring. According to Foresight News, the position rose to 135,951 shares in total.

data.title: China Gene-Editing Trial Death Draws Fire
data.description:
A young girl died after receiving an experimental gene-editing treatment in a clinical trial in Shanghai, Science reported, raising questions about regulatory oversight and transparency as China expands its biotechnology ambitions.

The case has drawn criticism over how the trial was supervised and disclosed.

data.title: Bond Investors Seek Above-7% Yield In $12 Billion Meta-Backed Data Center Deal
data.description: Bond investors are seeking a yield of more than 7% for a data center project in El Paso, Texas, backed by Meta and valued at $12 billion, according to 36Kr. The project, which has capacity of nearly 1 gigawatt, plans to issue bonds through a special-purpose vehicle affiliated with BlackRock. People familiar with the matter said pricing talks are still at an early stage and could change when the deal formally launches as soon as Monday.

data.title: Korean Stocks Sink as Chipmakers Fall on Middle East Tensions, China IPO
data.description:
South Korean stocks fell as funds cut exposure to chipmakers after overnight losses in US peers, with investors also weighing a Chinese rival’s imminent share sale and rising Middle East tensions, according to Bloomberg.

The slide in chipmakers helped drag the broader market lower as traders adjusted positions ahead of the IPO.

data.title: ANZ Maintains Brent Forecast at $92 a Barrel for Late Third Quarter of 2026
data.description: According to Jin10, ANZ maintained its forecast for Brent crude at $92 per barrel at the end of the third quarter of 2026, reflecting an uneven recovery in Persian Gulf oil flows.

data.title: CASHCAT Supply Concentration Exceeds 89% Among Top 1,000 Addresses
data.description: CASHCAT token holders have surpassed 61,000, while the top 1,000 addresses collectively hold about 89.1% of the supply, according to Arkham data analysis. According to ChainCatcher, those top addresses also hold other tokens on Robinhood Chain, including PONS, TENDIES, STONKBROKER, Index, WOOD, SQUEEZE, VIRTUAL, JUGGERNAUT, UP, DEGEN, KITSU, SWOGE, and VEX.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | India Markets Shrug Off Student Protests
data.description:
Student protests in India’s capital, a political headache for Prime Minister Narendra Modi, are not showing up in the country’s financial markets, according to Bloomberg.

The protests have so far failed to unsettle local assets.

data.title: Ripple Launches Ripple Mint Platform for RLUSD Stablecoin Management
data.description: Ripple has launched the Ripple Mint platform, which allows institutions to mint, redeem and manage RLUSD stablecoins through a UI or API. According to Odaily, the platform is designed for institutional users.

data.title: Ruben Gallego Says Democrats Will Counter CLARITY Act Ethics Draft
data.description:
Democratic Senator Ruben Gallego said he will work with Republicans on a counterproposal to the White House-approved ethics provisions in the CLARITY Act, saying the draft released this week was “not a serious effort.” The remarks came after Senate Republicans on Wednesday released proposed text for the Digital Asset Market Clarity (CLARITY) Act that included ethics language barring all US federal officials, including U.S. President Donald Trump, from issuing or sponsoring any digital asset. Democrats said the provisions fall short, while Republican Senator Bernie Moreno described the draft as containing “the most powerful ethics language in US history.” According to Cointelegraph, Gallego criticized the Republican-backed draft in comments to Politico on Thursday, saying it did not reflect the months of work both parties had done on the legislation.

Gallego said he would continue working with Senator Thom Tillis and other Republicans to produce revised language. He said the effort is still ongoing and that Democrats intend to send back their own proposal. “We are still in this fight,” Gallego told Politico. “We are going to send back language.”

data.title: U.S. Senator Ruben Gallego Plans Counterproposal on CLARITY Act Ethics Rules
data.description:
U.S. Democratic Senator Ruben Gallego said he will work with Republicans on a counterproposal to the ethics provisions in the CLARITY Act that the White House has endorsed. According to Odaily, he said the draft released this week was "not a serious proposal."

U.S. Senate Republicans released proposed CLARITY Act text on Wednesday. The ethics provisions would bar all U.S. federal officials, including U.S. President Donald Trump, from issuing or sponsoring any digital asset.

Democrats said the ethics provisions are not strong enough. Republican Senator Bernie Moreno rejected that view, saying the draft contains "the strongest ethics language in American history." Gallego said he will move the counterproposal forward with Senator Thom Tillis and other Republicans and submit new draft language.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Analyst Shawn Kim Turns Bearish on Memory Chips, Jukan Says
data.description:
Citrini analyst Jukan said on X that a Morgan Stanley research report circulating in the market shows analyst Shawn Kim has turned bearish on the memory chip sector. According to Odaily, the report said NAND module inventories have risen to about 13 weeks, demand has cooled, and fourth-quarter NAND price gains are expected to slow to about 5%, with spot prices falling for two consecutive months.

The report also said CXMT is rapidly expanding capacity and that supply and demand are gradually moving into balance. It added that if NAND weakens, DRAM should also be viewed bearish, and that HBM market growth is expected to be limited to about 40%.

Jukan said he had verified the report with sources and that Kim has held similar views recently. He also said this may be one reason the South Korea KOSPI index has continued to pull back in recent weeks.

data.title: Wheat Extends July Rally on Supply Fears
data.description:
Wheat resumed its July rally after a brief bout of profit-taking as escalating geopolitical tensions and worsening weather in key producing regions kept supply concerns elevated, according to Bloomberg.

The move followed renewed worries over output and deliveries, with war risks and adverse weather supporting prices.

data.title: Charter Communications Makes Debt Exchange Offer
data.description:
Charter Communications Inc. unveiled plans to exchange a number of its bonds for cash and new debt, according to Bloomberg.

The offer is part of a move to revamp the company’s debt stack.

data.title: ETH Leads Major Crypto Perpetual Futures Liquidations at $55.83 Million
data.description: ETH recorded $55.83 million in estimated liquidations over the past 24 hours among major crypto perpetual futures. According to NS3.AI, BTC followed with $49.26 million, while SOL saw $7.22 million in liquidations.
